Convert any Japanese era date (Reiwa, Heisei, Showa, Taisho, Meiji) to the Western Gregorian calendar — and back. Free, instant, works in your browser. No login required.

令和2年2020Olympic year (postponed)
令和3年2021Tokyo Olympics held
令和4年2022View details

令和6年2024View details
令和7年2025Current year
令和8年2026Next year

How to Use the Japanese Era Date Converter

Converting Japanese era dates to the Western calendar is simple with this free tool. Select your desired era from the dropdown — choose from Reiwa (令和), Heisei (平成), Showa (昭和), Taisho (大正), or Meiji (明治). Enter the era year number, and optionally specify the month and day for a complete date conversion. Click "Convert" to instantly see the corresponding Western (Gregorian) calendar date. You can also convert in reverse: switch to the "Western → Era" tab, enter any year from 1868 onward, and the tool will identify the correct Japanese era. The converter handles transition years automatically — for example, 2019 correctly shows as both Heisei 31 (January–April) and Reiwa 1 (May–December) when month and day are specified.

Understanding Japanese Era Dates (和暦・元号)

Japan uses a unique calendar system called gengō (元号) or wareki (和暦), where years are counted from the start of each imperial era. Each emperor's reign defines a new era, and approximately 68% of Japanese government documents, official forms, and legal paperwork use this era dating system alongside the Western calendar. The current era, Reiwa (令和, meaning "Beautiful Harmony"), began on May 1, 2019, when Emperor Naruhito ascended the throne. Before Reiwa, Japan was in the Heisei era (1989–2019), and before that the Showa era (1926–1989). Transition years require special attention: 2019 spans both Heisei 31 and Reiwa 1, while 1989 spans both Showa 64 and Heisei 1. The conversion formula is straightforward — simply add the era's offset to the era year. For Reiwa: year + 2018 = Western year. For Heisei: year + 1988. For Showa: year + 1925.

Frequently Asked Questions

What year is Reiwa 7 in Western calendar?

Reiwa 7 is the year 2025 in the Western (Gregorian) calendar. The Reiwa era began on May 1, 2019, so to convert any Reiwa year to the Western year, simply add 2018 to the Reiwa year number. For example, Reiwa 7 + 2018 = 2025. Use the converter above to instantly convert any Reiwa date.

What is the current Japanese era in 2025?

The current Japanese era is Reiwa (令和), which began on May 1, 2019, when Emperor Naruhito ascended to the Chrysanthemum Throne following the abdication of Emperor Akihito. The year 2025 corresponds to Reiwa 7 (令和7年) in the Japanese calendar system. Japan uses this era dating system alongside the Western calendar.

How do I convert Showa year to Western year?

To convert a Showa year to the Western calendar, add 1925 to the Showa year number. For example, Showa 64 equals 1925 + 64 = 1989. The Showa era spanned from December 25, 1926 to January 7, 1989, covering 64 years under Emperor Hirohito. Visit our dedicated Showa converter page for a complete reference table.

What is 元年 (gannen) in Japanese calendar?

元年 (gannen) means "first year" of a Japanese era. Instead of writing year 1, the Japanese calendar traditionally uses 元年 to mark the inaugural year of each new era. For example, Reiwa 元年 (Reiwa gannen) refers to 2019, the first year of the Reiwa era. Our converter accepts both 元 and 1 as valid year inputs.

What happened in the transition from Heisei to Reiwa?

The transition from Heisei to Reiwa occurred on May 1, 2019. April 30, 2019 was the final day of Heisei 31 (平成31年), when Emperor Akihito abdicated. May 1, 2019 became Reiwa 1 (令和元年) as Emperor Naruhito ascended. This was the first planned abdication in over 200 years of Japanese imperial history.
